  "judge_id": "esr_multi_anon",

  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The agent performed a comprehensive copy-edit. It fixed the original's run-on sentences, normalized verb tenses, removed conversational filler ('we are observing that'), and improved overall flow and professionalism. The result is a significant improvement in readability and polish, consistent with a careful human edit.",
    "RX.1": "The agent provided an excellent, dedicated 'Uncertainty' section. It explicitly noted that it did not verify factual claims (e.g., performance numbers, table references) and flagged a specific editorial decision (dropping a phrase about dataset curation) with its reasoning, which is a model for this dimension.",
    "RX.3": "The request was to 'Clean this up.' The agent performed a copy-edit, which is exactly what was requested. It did not go out of scope by summarizing, adding new information, or performing a different task.",
    "RX.4": "The agent's edits improve the text's quality without introducing any errors, fabrications, or regressions. The original text was not harmful, and the edited version remains harm-free.",
    "RX.5": "The agent provides a high-level summary of the types of edits it made and specifically calls out one potentially meaning-altering change in its uncertainty section. This is very good traceability. It falls short of a perfect 5 because not every non-cosmetic change is individually annotated, but it is much better than only tracing major rewrites."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.6": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
